data.description:
## Why
`_commands/fsck.py` crossed the 800-LOC soft cap (->816) when story 21dd added the store-compat wiring (the `_repair_ticket` fail-closed `except` and the read-only diagnostic's structured `compat_error` surfacing). It was grandfathered into the module-size allowlist/ceilings as a bounded change; this ticket tracks the real remedy.

## What
Split along the existing call-graph seam: extract the repair cluster (`_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_repair_run`, `_disable_schedule`/`_enable_schedule`) into a sibling `src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py`, leaving the diagnostic scan + `_transform_json` in `fsck.py`. Both halves must stay above the 100-LOC floor. Then graduate `fsck.py` off the allowlist + ceilings and remove its `docs/architecture.md` remedy row.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_commands/fsck.py` drops back under 800 LOC and is removed from the allowlist + ceilings files.
- [ ] Repair functionality unchanged (fsck --repair behaves identically); `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
- [ ] No file created under 100 LOC by the split.
data.title: Split _commands/fsck.py repair cluster into fsck_repair.py (module-size)
